title: Circle2D Object
description: Transient 2D circle. A transient circle is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient circles are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information. They are created statically using one of the create methods of the Circle2D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Circle2D object.

methods.name: createByCenter
methods.description: Creates a transient 2D circle object by specifying a center and radius.

methods.name: createByThreePoints
methods.description: Creates a transient 2D circle through three points.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the circle.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the circle.

properties.name: asNurbsCurve
properties.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the circle.

properties.name: center
properties.description: Gets and sets the center position of the circle.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the circle.

asNurbsCurve.title: Circle2D.asNurbsCurve Property
asNurbsCurve.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the circle.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circle2D_var.asNurbsCurve

center.title: Circle2D.center Property
center.description: Gets and sets the center position of the circle.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circle2D_var.center

# Set the value of the property.
circle2D_var.center = propertyValue

syntax.python:

isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circle2D_var.isValid

objectType.title: Circle2D.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.

radius.description: Gets and sets the radius of the circle.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circle2D_var.radius

# Set the value of the property.

classType.title: Circle2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Circle2D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Circle2D object.
copy.return_description: Returns an independent copy of this Circle2D object.

createByCenter.title: Circle2D.createByCenter Method
createByCenter.description: Creates a transient 2D circle object by specifying a center and radius.
createByCenter.return_description: Returns the new Circle2D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: A Point2D object that defines the center of the circle.

createByThreePoints.title: Circle2D.createByThreePoints Method
createByThreePoints.description: Creates a transient 2D circle through three points.
createByThreePoints.return_description: Returns the new Circle2D object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first point on the circle.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second point on the circle.

parameters.name: pointThree
parameters.description: The third point on the circle.

getData.title: Circle2D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the circle.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The output point defining the center position of the circle.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the circle.

set.title: Circle2D.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the circle.
set.return_description: Returns true if redefining the circle is successful

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: A point that defines the center position of the circle.

transformBy.title: Circle2D.transformBy Method
transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 2D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.
